commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared shell-command heuristic, cron add/edit stderr warnings for main-session systemEvent payloads, focused cron CLI tests, and a CHANGELOG entry.

PR surface: Source +74, Tests +126, Docs +1. Total +201 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main sends main-session shell-looking systemEvent payloads through cron.add/cron.update without warning, while the runtime enqueues the text as a system event rather than executing it.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated cleanup can remove the release-owned changelog entry; the cron implementation itself has no blocking correctness finding.

target.title: fix(cron): warn when --system-event on main session contains shell commands
target.body:
## Summary

- `cron add --system-event "python3 script.py" --session main` and similar commands used to create the job silently without warning that the script text will not be executed.
- Adds a shared `looksLikeShellCommand()` helper with a shell-command heuristic for common runtimes and relative script paths such as `./run.sh`.
- Emits a non-blocking `stderr` warning in both `cron add` and `cron edit` when the effective session target is `main`, the payload is a `systemEvent`, and the text looks like a shell command.

Fixes #63107

## Root Cause

System events on the main session are dispatched as context notifications to the agent. They are not executed as shell commands. Users who pass shell command syntax via `--system-event` on `--session main` get a silently non-functional job with no indication of the limitation.

## Changes

- `src/cli/cron-cli/shared.ts`: Adds the shared shell-command heuristic.
- `src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-add.ts`: Warns before the `cron.add` RPC for main-session shell-looking system events.
- `src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-edit.ts`: Warns before the `cron.update` RPC for explicit main-session edits and existing-main jobs edited without `--session`.
- `src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts` and `src/cli/cron-cli/shared.test.ts`: Cover add/edit warning paths, non-matches, `--session MAIN`, existing-main edit fallback, paginated existing-job lookup, and relative script paths.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Adds the Unreleased Fixes entry.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Main-session cron `--system-event` payloads that look like shell commands now warn the user before the gateway RPC, so `./run.sh` is no longer accepted silently as if it would execute.

Real environment tested: Local checkout of current PR head `a0e8878dfce40906653676ed616c71e2504bc33f` on 2026-05-26, running the OpenClaw source CLI through `node --import tsx src/entry.ts` with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, bundled plugins disabled, and a redacted gateway token.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Rebased the PR branch onto current `upstream/main`.
2. Resolved the `src/cli/cron-cli/shared.test.ts` and `CHANGELOG.md` conflicts.
3. Ran focused cron CLI/shared regression tests on the rebased head.
4. Ran the real OpenClaw CLI against an isolated temp home/state directory for both `cron add` and explicit-main `cron edit`.
5. Pointed the gateway URL at a deliberately closed local endpoint so the pre-RPC warning can be observed before the expected connection failure.

Focused regression tests on current head:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli/shared.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/antonio/projects/openclaw_worktrees/pr63112_clean

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  99 passed (99)
   Start at  15:32:04
   Duration  1.31s (transform 951ms, setup 273ms, import 780ms, tests 131ms, environment 0ms)
```

Whitespace check on current head:

```console
$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
(no output)
```

Evidence after fix: copied terminal output from the current-head real CLI path for both `cron add` and explicit-main `cron edit`.

CLI proof for `cron add`:

```console
$ node scripts/run-with-env.mjs \
  OPENCLAW_HOME=<isolated-state> \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<isolated-state> \
  OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
  -- node --import tsx src/entry.ts cron add \
    --name proof-shell-warning \
    --every 1h \
    --session main \
    --system-event ./run.sh \
    --url ws://127.0.0.1:9 \
    --token proof-redacted \
    --timeout 1000

Warning: --system-event on --session main does not execute shell commands.
  The text is dispatched as a notification to the main agent session only.
  To run a script, use: --message "..." --session isolated --wake now
GatewayTransportError: gateway closed (1006 abnormal closure (no close frame)): no close reason
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:9
Source: cli --url
Config: <isolated-state>/openclaw.json
```

CLI proof for explicit-main `cron edit`:

```console
$ node scripts/run-with-env.mjs \
  OPENCLAW_HOME=<isolated-state> \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<isolated-state> \
  OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
  -- node --import tsx src/entry.ts cron edit proof-job \
    --session main \
    --system-event ./deploy.sh \
    --url ws://127.0.0.1:9 \
    --token proof-redacted \
    --timeout 1000

Warning: --system-event on --session main does not execute shell commands.
  The text is dispatched as a notification to the main agent session only.
  To run a script, use: --message "..." --session isolated --wake now
GatewayTransportError: gateway closed (1006 abnormal closure (no close frame)): no close reason
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:9
Source: cli --url
Config: <isolated-state>/openclaw.json
```

Observed result: The warning is emitted before the gateway RPC fails for both `cron add` and explicit-main `cron edit`, confirming the real CLI path warns users before attempting to create or update the job. The gateway was intentionally pointed at a local closed endpoint for this proof; the behavior under test is the pre-RPC user warning.

What was not tested: A successful `cron.add` or `cron.update` against a live authenticated gateway was not required for this fix because the changed behavior is the pre-RPC warning path. The focused regression suite covers the `cron edit` warning path for edits to an existing main-session job without an explicit `--session`, including paginated existing-job lookup.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli/shared.test.ts`
- [x] `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- [x] Current-head real CLI proof above shows `cron add` and explicit-main `cron edit` print the warning before the gateway RPC.
target.author: liaoandi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
